items.text: Donate Blood, Save Lives! Campus Blood Drive – Tuesday, March 18, 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM in Student Union Ballroom. All donors receive a free T-shirt and a $5 meal voucher. Walk-ins welcome. Bring your student ID.

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what do donors receive?

options.A: A $5 meal voucher and a free water bottle
options.B: A free T-shirt and a $10 gift card
options.C: A $5 meal voucher and a free gym pass
options.D: A free T-shirt and a $5 meal voucher

questions.explanation: The flyer states that all donors receive a free T-shirt and a $5 meal voucher.

questions.stem: What must donors bring to the blood drive?
questions.explanation: The flyer says 'Bring your student ID.'

items.text: Notice: Campus Shuttle Detour. Due to construction on Elm Street, the Blue Line shuttle will follow an alternate route from March 3 to March 7. Temporary stops are located at South Lot B and Anderson Hall. Regular service resumes March 8.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where are the temporary shuttle stops located?

options.A: Elm Street and the main campus entrance
options.B: South Lot B and the Student Union
options.C: South Lot B and Anderson Hall

questions.explanation: The notice states that temporary stops are at South Lot B and Anderson Hall.

questions.stem: When will the regular shuttle schedule be restored?
questions.explanation: The notice indicates that regular service resumes on March 8.

items.text: Dear James, this is a reminder that the mandatory lab safety training for Chemistry 101 is this Thursday, March 20, at 2:00 PM in Room 204 of the Science Center. Please arrive 10 minutes early. Best, Dr. Park

questions.stem: What time is the lab safety training scheduled to start?
questions.explanation: The email says the training is at 2:00 PM.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the training?

options.A: It is optional for chemistry students
options.B: It will be held in a different building
options.C: Attendance is required for the course
options.D: It has been rescheduled to a later date

questions.explanation: The email describes it as 'mandatory lab safety training for Chemistry 101,' so attendance is required.

items.text: Dear Maria, your resume review appointment with Career Services is confirmed for Friday, April 11, at 10:30 AM in Johnson Hall, Room 205. Bring a printed copy. There is a $5 fee payable by credit card upon arrival. Best, Career Services

questions.stem: How must the $5 fee for the appointment be paid?
questions.explanation: The email specifies that the fee is payable by credit card upon arrival.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the resume review appointment?

options.A: It requires the student to bring a physical copy
options.B: It is free of charge for all students
options.C: It can be rescheduled to another day
options.D: It will last for two hours

questions.explanation: The email asks to 'Bring a printed copy,' so the student must bring a physical copy.
